description: number of men In the engineering branch is allowed by the new charter and to that should be added the long and Indispensable list of inspectors and their assistants chainmen linemen axemen blacksmiths blacksmiths helpers carpenters firemen foremen of different grades painters pavers laborers and the like 1he outline of the work allotted to this commissioner In Section 524 of the new charter shows plainly enough the enormous amount of business which has been relegated to this commlgnlmiOT For example the extensive work formerly done by the Commissioner of Street Improvements c the Twentythird and Twentyfourth Words of New York in regulating grading regrading curbing and flagging and guttering of streets laying cross walks the constructing and repairing of public roads paving repairing and repaying of all streets and the relaying of all pavements reipoved for any cause is devolved upon this Commissioner of Highways Not alone that but all similar work in Brooklyn in Long Island City and it suburbs and in the corporated towns and villages of Staten Island also takes its place upon this schedule An indication of what this great work may involve when carried out over the newly acquired territory is had from the budget
